question.text: Vinod, an investment adviser, holds shares in Company ABC. A client asks for advice on Company ABC. Under SEBI regulations, Vinod must:

answer.text: Sell his shares in Company ABC before providing any advice
Disclose his holding or position in Company ABC to the client before advising
Refuse to advise on Company ABC to avoid conflict of interest
Provide advice without disclosure since his personal holdings are private matters

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI IA Regulations require that an IA shall disclose to the client its holding or position, if any, in the financial products or securities which are subject matter of advice. **The Trap:** The IA doesn't need to sell the holding or refuse to advise — disclosure is the remedy, not avoidance. Personal holdings that are subject matter of advice are NOT private matters. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XV has similar disclosure requirements for research analysts regarding financial interest in subject companies. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Maintain a pre-trade disclosure register — before advising on any security you hold, ensure the disclosure is documented and acknowledged by the client.
